diagrams.title: 0/1 Knapsack DP Table Structure
diagrams.description: 2D table showing DP[i][w] states for items and capacities
elements: Row 0: base case (0 items) - all zeros
Cell (i,w): max value using first i items with capacity w
Arrows showing recurrence: either from above (exclude) or from left-up (include)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding DP state transitions and table filling order

diagrams.title: Subset Sum DP Table
diagrams.description: Boolean table showing achievable subset sums
elements: TRUE/FALSE cells showing if sum is achievable
Trace path from bottom-right to find subset
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing subset selection and base case initialization

diagrams.title: Space-Optimized Knapsack Visualization
elements: Single row of cells representing capacity 0 to W
Backward arrow showing direction W to 0
Color coding: original values (from previous item) vs updated values
Demonstrates why backward prevents item reuse
diagrams.purpose: Understanding space optimization and reverse traversal necessity

diagrams.title: Comparison: Greedy vs DP for Knapsack
diagrams.description: Example showing why greedy fails for 0/1 Knapsack
elements: Greedy: takes item1 (ratio=6), then can't take item2 - value=60
DP optimal: takes item2 only - value=100
Visual comparison showing lower greedy result
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why DP/greedy difference matters and when greedy fails
